reason: Part A §2.9 directly and verbatim answers: Razorpay not responsible for refund requests; merchant's sole responsibility.
Part B Part IA §1.1 directly answers both parts of the question — which section governs offline PA/Devices, and that it is supplementary (not replacing) Part A/Part I.
Part A §3.2 verbatim: statutory variations in applicable taxes during subsistence of Terms shall be borne by You (the merchant).
Part B Part IB §1.19 (LRS) verbatim: you must immediately notify Razorpay in writing of any regulatory notice on LRS transactions.
Part A §2.1 verbatim: merchant responsible for unauthorized access/fraudulent transactions from Secure Credentials compromise; Razorpay not liable.
Part B Part I §1(f) verbatim: Razorpay may deduct at its sole discretion from Transaction Amount or other funds for amounts charged to it or debited by Facility Providers.

Cited clause Part B Part IB §1.13 (INR 25 lakh cap) applies only to PA-CB Outward (cross-border) transactions. Question describes domestic-sounding enterprise invoice scenario; ToS is actually silent on a domestic per-transaction cap. Citation doesn't unambiguously answer without assuming cross-border.
Part B Part I §7.1 verbatim addresses both parts: Razorpay's role as SaaS (not PSP) and merchant indemnity for payment-gateway/aggregator disputes arising from Optimizer.
Part A §9.3 verbatim: merchant must forthwith reimburse card-network fines; Razorpay may suspend settlement or suspend/terminate Services forthwith.
Part A §14.3 verbatim: Razorpay may update fees including for previously free services; use constitutes consent.
Part A §2.18 verbatim: 10-year retention of transaction records from date of order placed on site.
Part A §3.1 verbatim: Razorpay PA fees include zero MDR for Rupay Debit Cards and UPI transactions.
Part B Part III §1 verbatim: TokenHQ complies with RBI tokenisation Circular and the Circular is deemed included in Applicable Laws.
Part A §2.20 verbatim: merchant bears all losses Razorpay incurs from convenience fees and must indemnify Razorpay.
Definitions: Facility Providers verbatim includes card networks and banks issuing credit cards.
Axis (charge_type: tax vs. late-payment interest) is not load-bearing; both clauses apply concurrently to the question, and a direct Cat A answer listing both satisfies it.
Axis (Fee Credits vs. Transaction Amount deduction) does not change the liability cap — §7.2 sets the cap at one month's fees regardless of payment method. Not load-bearing.
Both 'timing of refund relative to termination' branches arrive at the same 'yes, fees still owed' answer. Axis is not load-bearing; Cat A answerable.
Duplicate structural flaw of B-001 — 'additional charges on top of stated fees' admits both clauses concurrently; no fork needed.
Question explicitly asks both sub-questions ('late charges AND tax treatment'). A direct Cat A answer covering both clauses satisfies it; no context is missing.
Duplicate structural flaw of B-001 — the two clauses apply concurrently; question is a direct Cat A.
Recovery process genuinely forks: §2.25 (penalty via settlement deduction, no deadline) vs. §2.3 (chargeback deduction then 7-day debit-note). Axis is load-bearing and specific.

§2.25 is not triggered by 'failure to pay an amount owed' — it is triggered by a third-party imposing a penalty on Razorpay. Only §1.4(c)(i) late-interest actually responds to the question; axis is forced.

'Compliance issue' is genuinely broad; reconciliation-discrepancy vs. card-network non-compliance produce materially different outcomes (Razorpay liability disclaimer vs. explicit suspension/termination rights). Load-bearing.
Question explicitly asks both sub-questions ('extra charges AND tax treatment of those charges'); no missing context. Direct Cat A.

Question specifically frames a customer contesting a convenience fee. The 'reconciliation_discrepancy' branch is a stretched alternative not supported by the question text. Direct Cat A §2.20 answer is correct.
Duplicate structural flaw of B-001 — recycled tax-vs-late-interest split on 'additional charges on top of base fees'.

Second branch is a fabricated edge case — §1(h) deals with pre-settlement withholding and source-account refunds, not merchant-to-customer refunds. §3.4 directly answers the question as asked. Direct Cat A.
Duplicate structural flaw of B-001 — fifth iteration of the tax-vs-late-interest split.
Second branch ('right to issue refund', §3.1) answers a different question than the one asked. The fee-liability question is directly answered by §3.4 as Cat A; axis is not load-bearing.
ToS uses purely qualitative language ('reasonable grounds') without numeric thresholds for e-mandate fraud suspicion. Genuine vagueness; answer escalates appropriately.
§9.3 creates reimbursement obligation but is silent on merchant audit/contest rights, evidence requirements, or dispute mechanisms. Genuine silence.
Part B Part IV §1 is silent on the intermediate AFA-succeeded-but-first-transaction-failed scenario — no retry policy, no validity window. Genuine silence.

§4.3 references 'the Terms' and §4.2 defers to RBI circulars; ToS does not define precedence hierarchy between Razorpay's Terms and external RBI/NPCI rules. Borderline (§2.1–§2.4 do have concrete mechanics), but the precedence question genuinely is unaddressed.
§3.2 places tax variations on merchant but is silent on notice period, cap, calculation methodology, and dispute mechanism. Genuine silence on the operational mechanics.
§2.2 uses 'reasonable grounds to suspect' without defining threshold, checklist, cure period, or procedural safeguards. Genuine vague language.

§16.2 ties notice obligation to 'Applicable Laws' without defining them; §16.3 addresses some post-termination obligations but ToS is silent on wind-down rights, in-flight transactions, and fund-release timeline. Genuine silence.
§4.2 explicitly defers to two named RBI circulars plus 'other notifications... from time to time'. Classic external deferral without internal procedural specification.
§1.5 says 'if required' and defers to 'prevailing Indian income tax laws'; no rate, TDS section, or DTAA-eligibility process specified. External deferral.
§3.1 grants Razorpay 'sole discretion' to update fees with no notice period, no cap, no reasonableness qualifier. Genuine silence on merchant protections.
Thematic duplicate of C-004 — same §4.3 deferral question. Precedence of RBI/NPCI over Razorpay's Terms is genuinely undefined. Borderline as with C-004.

§1.1 prohibits derivative works but doesn't define the boundary for white-label/co-branded UI; RBI PA guidelines interaction with IP restrictions is also undefined. Genuine external deferral plus silence.
Thematic duplicate of C-008 — post-settlement fraud dispute defers to same RBI circulars. Genuine external deferral.
Thematic duplicate of C-005 — §3.2 on tax pass-through; silent on cap, notice, dispute mechanism. Genuine silence.
Thematic duplicate of C-012 — §1.1 white-label/IP ownership question; RBI PA/NPCI interaction with IP restrictions undefined. Genuine external deferral.
